HomePage.title: A more refined presence for menu, table and shared bill.
HomePage.description: Sazono connects digital menu, QR ordering, floor operations and payments on the same table with a calmer, warmer interface that stays readable in any theme.
HomePage.stats_hybrid: guest and waiter in the same session
HomePage.preview_description: Guests can order from QR while the waiter adds rounds and the kitchen receives separate tickets by station.
HomePage.preview_client_desc: Scan QR, build a cart and pay without downloading an app.
HomePage.preview_hall_desc: The waiter adds new rounds and keeps control of table closure.
HomePage.pillar_menu_desc: Photos, languages, availability and editable categories without relying on technical support.
HomePage.pillar_qr_desc: The guest enters by table, builds the order and pays before production when the flow requires it.
HomePage.pillar_bill_desc: The table keeps a single operational bill even when several guests pay at different times.
HomePage.journey_title: Sazono thinks of the table as a cycle, not as isolated sales.
HomePage.journey_description: The table session opens the context. Orders can come from QR or from the waiter. Kitchen works by station. The bill stays unified until the restaurant chooses to close it manually.
HomePage.journey_1: The restaurant publishes its own menu by branch.
HomePage.journey_2: The table opens a session from QR or from the waiter.
HomePage.journey_3: Orders travel to kitchen and bar according to each item's station.
HomePage.journey_4: The bill can be paid fully or partially without creating parallel bills.
HomePage.surfaces_description: Each surface solves a different problem. Guests need clarity. Staff needs speed. Cashier and kitchen need control.
HomePage.surface_qr_desc: Mobile-first menu, prepaid ordering and clear split bill.
HomePage.surface_staff_desc: Active table, fast rounds and manual closure when the balance reaches zero.
HomePage.surface_kitchen_desc: Separate station tickets without breaking the guest-facing commercial experience.
HomePage.surface_cashier_desc: Debt handling, walkouts and payment traceability.
HomePage.foundation_title: Visual foundation ready to grow into QR, staff and menu builder.
HomePage.foundation_description: This base already settles palette, dark mode and typography hierarchy. From here we can build QR and staff without remaking the visual system.
HomePage.foundation_item_4: Zod and React Hook Form for forms

QrPage.title: Guest QR experience
QrPage.description: This surface will be mobile first and visual. The digital menu, cart, prepayment and split bill will live here.
QrPage.placeholder: Pending implementation: published menu, QR cart, prepaid checkout and open bill payment.

StaffPage.title: Floor operations workspace
StaffPage.description: This first iteration connects to the real `floor` slice from the backend: create tables, list the branch floor, open manual sessions and resume the active session.

FloorConsole.requireLoginTitle: You need an active staff session
FloorConsole.requireLoginDescription: The floor flow reuses the same session from the admin workspace. Sign in first with a `staff` profile and then come back here.
FloorConsole.wrongProfileTitle: Your current session is not staff
FloorConsole.wrongProfileDescription: The `floor` backend only accepts internal restaurant profiles. Switch to a `staff` profile from the admin workspace.
FloorConsole.noRolesTitle: You still have no branch roles
FloorConsole.noRolesDescription: Before operating tables, this user needs active roles on at least one branch.
FloorConsole.refreshTitle: We couldn't refresh your staff profile
FloorConsole.refreshDescription: An unexpected error occurred while syncing the current session.
FloorConsole.workspaceDescription: From here staff can prepare the initial table catalog, open an internal session and resume operations on an occupied table.
FloorConsole.sourceHint: The allowed source depends on your current roles in the branch.
FloorConsole.readForbiddenTitle: Your current role can't operate floor
FloorConsole.readForbiddenDescription: To list tables or resume sessions you need `ADMIN`, `SUPERVISOR`, `WAITER` or `CASHIER` on the selected branch.
FloorConsole.createDescription: This action creates the table with its `qrToken` and leaves it available to open a session.
FloorConsole.createForbidden: Only `ADMIN` or `SUPERVISOR` can create tables on this branch.
FloorConsole.capacityHint: Use a reference capacity for floor and QR.
FloorConsole.sessionTitle: Current session for the focused table
FloorConsole.sessionDescription: When a table is already occupied, we can request the live detail of the active session and continue from there.
FloorConsole.sessionError: We couldn't retrieve the session detail.
FloorConsole.sessionEmpty: Select an occupied table to see its active session. If a table is available, you can open it first from the list below.
FloorConsole.tablesDescription: Operational list from `GET /api/v1/floor/tables`, including status, capacity, QR token and active session when present.
FloorConsole.tablesErrorDescription: Check permissions, session and selected branch.
FloorConsole.tablesEmpty: There are no tables yet in this branch. The natural next step is to create the first one.

AdminPage.title: Administrative onboarding connected to the backend
AdminPage.description: From here we can already operate login, restaurant bootstrap, first branch creation and basic staff management using real backend endpoints.

AdminLogin.title: Enter the real administrative flow for Sazono
AdminLogin.description: This workspace already talks to auth, restaurant bootstrap, branch creation and staff management. The client calls {path} and Next proxies server-side to the backend monolith.
AdminLogin.step2: 2. If you are platform, you can bootstrap a restaurant.
AdminLogin.step3: 3. If you are the first admin without roles yet, you can create the first branch.
AdminLogin.step4: 4. If you already are a branch admin, you can manage staff.
AdminLogin.profileHint: If the same identity can sign in with multiple profiles, the backend needs this selection.
AdminLogin.restaurantHint: Only if the identity has more than one staff profile

AdminBootstrap.title: Create restaurant and first admin account
AdminBootstrap.name: Display name
AdminBootstrap.passwordHint: This user will later sign in as restaurant `staff`.
AdminBootstrap.expectedDescription: The backend creates the base identity, restaurant and initial `staff_user`.

AdminBranch.title: Create the first branch and close the admin transitional state
AdminBranch.description: When the first restaurant admin still has no branch roles, this flow creates the branch and assigns `ADMIN` automatically.
AdminBranch.name: Branch name
AdminBranch.paymentHint: The backend currently accepts a string. The recommended default is `prepaid_order`.
AdminBranch.after3: 3. Your staff profile now shows the `ADMIN` role.

AdminStaff.title: Create internal users per branch
AdminStaff.description: The backend already allows listing staff and creating users with active roles per branch, reusing the base identity if the email already exists.
AdminStaff.passwordHint: If the email already exists in `auth.users`, the backend can reuse the base identity and this field becomes optional.

AdminConsole.branchSuccess: Branch {name} created and ADMIN role assigned.
AdminConsole.refreshTitle: We couldn't refresh the current profile
AdminConsole.forbiddenTitle: Staff profile connected, but without admin permissions
AdminConsole.forbiddenDescription: This workspace currently covers administrative onboarding and staff management. Your profile has operational access, but no branch ADMIN role.
AdminConsole.noRoles: This staff user still has no branch roles. The next natural step is to create the first branch.
